title: Marie-Soleil Landry
content:
Partner | Tax Group Regional Practice Lead - Montréal
People.ContactEmail mslandry@mccarthy.ca
Recognized for her innovative, practical, and business-focused approach, Marie-Soleil delivers tangible solutions and expert advice meeting the most complex tax challenges.
Marie-Soleil is partner in the firm’s National Tax Group in Montréal and the Practice Lead of our Tax Group in Québec. She maintains a practice focused on corporate taxation and strategic tax planning. She analyzes and develop complex strategies in connection with a variety of commercial transactions, including public and private mergers and acquisitions, and cross-border reorganizations. Whatever the tax issue, Marie-Soleil skillfully navigates the complexity to deliver results.
Marie-Soleil acted as tax counsel in the following mandates:
- National Bank of Canada in its C$1.0 billion issuance of subscription receipts and its C$5.0 billion acquisition of Canadian Western Bank;
- Desjardins General Insurance Group Inc. in its acquisition of all of ICPEI Holdings Inc.'s issued and outstanding shares
- Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec in their minority investment in AVENIR GLOBAL
- National Bank of Canada in its acquisition of the commercial loan portfolio of Silicon Valley Bank's Canadian branch
- Feldan Therapeutics in its US$21 million Series B investment round co-led by Genesys Capital and Fonds de solidarité FTQ
- The Special Committee of the Board of Directors of Neighbourly Pharmacy Inc. in its C$900 million sale of all issued and outstanding common shares for C$18.50 per share plus one contingent value right per Common Share to newly formed entity of Persistence Capital Partners, T.I.D Acquisition Corp.
- The underwriters as part of Milestone Pharmaceuticals in its pricing of C$30 Million public offering of common shares and pre-funded warrants
- Minafin Group in acquiring Delmar in Montreal, Quebec, strengthening its Healthcare Chemicals division
- Feldan Bio Inc. in closing a C$16.5 million Series B investment round to bring breakthrough treatments to clinical development
- Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec in its agreement to support the sale of Héroux-Devtek Inc. to Platinum Equity for C$1.35B
- Sagard Private Equity Canada in its investment in Groupe Norbec Inc.
- M.A.C. Métal Architectural Inc. in its acquisition by Cornerstone Building Brands
- McInnis Cement in its C$500 million private capital refinancing for the construction of a cement plant and several marine terminals totaling C$1.5 billion

#### Information Technology and Medias
- Sharethrough Inc. in the sale of its issued and outstanding shares to Equativ
- Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec on its definitive US$6.3 billion arrangement agreement to roll over the majority of its equity in Nuvei Corporation to Advent International
- Silver Lake , as Canadian counsel, in Vantage Data Centers' US$6.4 billion equity investment led by investment vehicles managed by DigitalBridge Group, Inc. and Silver Lake
- Levio Consulting with the acquisition strategy by CDPQ for C$125 million
- Corus Entertainment Inc. in its sale of Toon Boom Animation Inc. to Integrated Media Company for C$142 million
- TrackTik Software Inc. in connection with its sale to Trackforce Valiant
- Fresh Prep Foods in its acquisition of the assets of Montreal-based meal kit company Cook It Recipes Inc. in a distressed M&A transaction
- Corby Spirit and Wine in its 90% stake acquisition of Ace Beverage Group for C$148.5 million
- Nestlé S.A. in its agreement to acquire core brands of The Bountiful Company for US$5.75B
- Rio Tinto Alcan in its acquisition of a 50% stake in Matalco for US$700 million to form an aluminium recycling joint venture with Giampaolo Group
- Gold Fields Limited in its C$675 million acquisition of 50% interest in a partnership with Osisko Mining Inc. to develop Windfall project in Québec
- Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec and Trencap LP in the C$1.14 billion acquisition of the stake of Enbridge in Noverco Inc. and in the acquisition of the minority stake of BCI and RRUQ in Trencap
- Champion Iron in its refinancing and acquisition of 100% of Bloom Lake for C$650 million
- Ivanhoé Cambridge with its sale of Conestoga Mall to Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust for C$270 million and related investment by Ivanhoé in securities of Primaris REIT
- Le Groupe Maurice in its partnership with US-based Ventas Inc. through the sale of 85% interest in its Canadian senior housing portfolio
Marie-Soleil maintains an active presence in the tax community. She has published various articles, spoken on a variety of taxation topics and assisted in writing and editing tax publications in collaboration with Thompson Reuters.
Marie-Soleil holds a Bachelor of Laws degree from Université Laval (2010) and a Master’s degree in Taxation from the Université de Sherbrooke. She is a member of the Canadian Bar Association, the Young Bar Association of Montreal, the Canadian International Fiscal Association and the Canadian Tax Foundation. Marie-Soleil was called to the Quebec Bar in 2012.
